La La Land (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $151,101,803Details
International Box Office $352,751,172
Worldwide Box Office $503,852,975
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,688,871
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $10,981,500
Total $18,670,371
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Sebastian and Mia are drawn together by their common desire to do what they love. But as success mounts they are faced with decisions that begin to fray the fragile fabric of their love affair, and the dreams they worked so hard to maintain in each other threaten to rip them apart.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$881,104 (0.6% of total gross)
Legs:10.40 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:30.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 16.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/3,236 max. theaters, 7.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $202,997,188

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 9th, 2016 (Limited) by Lionsgate
International Releases: November 30th, 2016 (Wide), released as LA LA LAND (South Korea)
December 9th, 2016 (Wide), released as Kalifornijos svajos (Lithuania)
December 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Taiwan)
December 16th, 2016 (Wide) (New Zealand)
December 16th, 2016 (Wide) (India)
Video Release: April 11th, 2017 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some language.
(Rating bulletin 2436 (Cert #50363), 8/3/2016)
Running Time: 128 minutes
Award nomination: 2017 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Film Winner, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Musical or Comedy, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Hollywood
Plot point: Aspiring Actress / Actor, Big Break, Musicians, Non-Chronological, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance
Social setting: Jazz, Movie Business, Music Industry, On Tour
Subgenre: Romantic Musical
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Gilbert Films, Imposter Pictures, Summit Entertainment, Black Label Media, TIK Films, Marc Platt Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Sebastian and Mia are drawn together by their common desire to do what they love. But as success mounts they are faced with decisions that begin to fray the fragile fabric of their love affair, and the dreams they worked so hard to maintain in each other threaten to rip them apart.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$881,104 (0.6% of total gross)
Legs:10.40 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:30.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 16.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/3,236 max. theaters, 7.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $202,997,188

MPA Rating: PG-13 for some language.

Running Time: 128 minutes
Award nomination: 2017 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
